name: Direct-detection limits on WIMP-nucleon cross section (LZ, XENONnT)
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict a spin-independent WIMP-nucleon elastic-scattering cross section below the LUX-ZEPLIN (LZ) and XENONnT bounds. LZ 2024 sets sigma_SI < 9.2 x 10^-48 cm^2 at the most sensitive WIMP mass m_chi ~ 36 GeV. Spin-dependent bounds at the 10^-42 - 10^-41 cm^2 level depending on target nucleon.

regime.description: Galactic-halo WIMP velocities (v ~ 10^-3 c), recoil energies keV; sensitive to dark-matter-quark or dark-matter-gluon couplings.

current_bound.units: cm^2 (90% CL upper limit, sigma_SI at m_chi ~ 36 GeV)
current_bound.reference: LZ Collaboration, 'First Dark Matter Search Results from the LUX-ZEPLIN (LZ) Experiment', PRL 131, 041002 (2023), arXiv:2207.03764

what_it_excludes: Closes large parts of the parameter space for thermal-relic WIMPs in the 10 GeV - 1 TeV range with electroweak-scale couplings: most natural SUSY neutralinos, generic Higgs-portal scalars, and Z'-mediated dark matter. Approaching the irreducible 'neutrino floor' from coherent solar/atmospheric/diffuse-supernova neutrino scattering.

parameterization.name: Non-relativistic effective theory of DM-nucleon scattering: 14 operators (Anand-Fitzpatrick-Haxton) capturing all standard couplings; mapped to particle-physics models via SMEFT / DM-EFT matching.
parameterization.reference: Anand, Fitzpatrick, Haxton, PRC 89, 065501 (2014), arXiv:1308.6288; Goodman et al., PRD 82, 116010 (2010) (DM-EFT for colliders).
parameterization.summary: DM-nucleon scattering rate: R = (rho_chi / m_chi) * sigma_SI * <v> * eta(v_min), with sigma_SI converted from particle-level cross section via Helm form factor.

procedural.predicted_observable: Recoil-energy spectrum dR/dE in xenon, expressed as sigma_SI(m_chi) at fixed momentum transfer.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Operator-dependent. Standard SI: sigma_SI < 9.2 x 10^-48 cm^2 at 36 GeV.
framework_inputs: If the framework includes a stable DM particle with mass and couplings to quarks/gluons, compute sigma_SI(m_chi) using DM-EFT matching.
Include nuclear form factors (Helm, or shell-model) for spin-dependent couplings.

primary_references.citation: LZ Collaboration, 'First Dark Matter Search Results from the LUX-ZEPLIN (LZ) Experiment', PRL 131, 041002 (2023)
XENONnT Collaboration, 'First Dark Matter Search with Nuclear Recoils from the XENONnT Experiment', PRL 131, 041003 (2023)
